title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: QALY calculations sensitive utility weights discount rates. extreme conditions, QALY outcomes can predicted exactly.

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: discount rate extremely high (e.g. 1.01.0, representing 100%100\% discount rate), future value discounted zero immediately. testhta, discount rate 1 greater sets cycle values 0. expect total QALYs equal 0.
code: data(test_data)  check_model_qalys(   expected_qalys = 0,   data = test_data,   discount_rate = 1,   label = "T01: QALYs are 0 when discount rate is 100%" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: Similarly, simulate infinite discount rate (e.g. discount_rate = 1000), QALY gains must tend zero.
code: check_model_qalys(   expected_qalys = 0,   data = test_data,   discount_rate = 1000,   label = "T08: QALYs tend to 0 under infinite discounting" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: health state utilities set 0, QALYs can accumulated, regardless survival discounting. can test setting utility values 0:
code: check_model_qalys(   expected_qalys = 0,   data = test_data,   u_healthy = 0,   u_sick = 0,   label = "T04: QALYs are 0 when all utility inputs are 0" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: Similar QALYs, cost calculations must equal zero specific extreme parameters.

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: health state occupancy costs, transition costs, intervention costs set 0, model must output total cost 0.
code: check_model_costs(   expected_costs = 0,   data = test_data,   c_healthy = 0,   c_sick = 0,   c_intervention = 0,   c_death = 0,   label = "T06: Costs are 0 when all cost parameters are 0" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: infinite cost discount rate, costs incurred time 0 discounted zero. testhta treats discount rates ≥1\ge 1 zeroing cycles, assert:
code: check_model_costs(   expected_costs = 0,   data = test_data,   discount_rate = 1000,   label = "T12: Costs tend to 0 under infinite discounting" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: Survival transitions govern population trace. can verify survival calculations setting transition probabilities death extreme bounds.

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: probability transitioning “Dead” state set 1 alive states, cohort die immediately first cycle. Since cohort lives cycle 1, life expectancy must equal exactly 1.
code: check_model_le(   expected_le = 1,   data = test_data,   p_healthy_death = 1,   p_sick_death = 1,   label = "T05: Life expectancy is 1 when death rate is 100%" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: probability death set 0, member cohort can die. Thus, entire cohort must survive full time horizon (n_cycles). set time horizon 50 cycles, life expectancy must exactly 50.
code: check_model_le(   expected_le = 50,   data = test_data,   p_healthy_death = 0,   p_sick_death = 0,   n_cycles = 50,   label = "T10: Life expectancy equals time horizon when death rate is 0%" )

title: Writing Basic Bounding Tests
text: utilizing static bounding tests, can guarantee mathematical limits model preserved. tests form bedrock verification suite, ensuring simple arithmetic mistakes indexing shifts pass undetected.

title: Introduction to testhta
text: core function testhta ce_markov(), runs cohort Markov model. model expects: - start_pop: Initial counts cohort across health states. - p_matrix: 3-dimensional array transition probabilities (states ×\times states ×\times treatments). - state_c_matrix: matrix costs state occupancy. - trans_c_matrix: matrix costs incurred state transitions. - state_q_matrix: matrix quality--life weights (utilities) state. package includes built-baseline dataset called test_data. Let’s inspect structure: contains: - start_pop: Vector length 3 (Asymptomatic disease, Progressive disease, Dead) - p_matrix: 3×3×23 \times 3 \times 2 array without_drug with_drug - state_c_matrix: State cost matrix treatment - state_q_matrix: Utility matrix treatment - discount_rate: Standard HTA discount rate (typically 3.5% 0.035) - n_cycles: Time horizon model
code: # Load baseline dataset data(test_data) names(test_data) #>  [1] "start_pop"      "p_matrix"       "state_c_matrix" "trans_c_matrix" #>  [5] "state_q_matrix" "n_cycles"       "init_age"       "s_names"        #>  [9] "t_names"        "discount_rate"

title: Introduction to testhta
text: can run model passing test_data parameters directly ce_markov(), using wrapper helper run_model(). execute Markov simulation: output run_model() list containing population distributions, cycle-specific costs QALYs, summary metrics.
code: # Run with the helper wrapper results <- run_model(test_data)

title: Introduction to testhta
text: testhta provides clean getter functions extract key outcomes model results: get_qalys(): Extracts total Quality-Adjusted Life Years (QALYs). get_costs(): Extracts total costs. get_le(): Extracts total Life Expectancy (undiscounted life-years). get_icer(): Calculates Incremental Cost-Effectiveness Ratio (ICER) two arms. Let’s retrieve values results:
code: # Get total QALYs get_qalys(results) #> without_drug    with_drug  #>     9.048517     9.589129  # Get total Costs get_costs(results) #> without_drug    with_drug  #>     9205.744    16977.576  # Get life expectancy get_le(results) #> without_drug    with_drug  #>     12.06416     12.68332  # Calculate the ICER get_icer(results) #> [1] 14375.98

title: Introduction to testhta
text: package provides setter functions follow tidy workflow using base R pipe (|>). allow easily modify input parameters scenario sensitivity analyses running model: set_discount_rate(input, discount_rate) set_time_horizon(input, n_cycles) set_costs(input, scenario, values) set_utilities(input, scenario, values) example, calculate ICER discounting shorter time horizon (20 cycles): piping framework makes scenario analysis clean, readable, less prone copy-paste errors.
code: scenario_results <- test_data |>   set_discount_rate(0) |>   set_time_horizon(20) |>   run_model()  get_icer(scenario_results) #> [1] 7392.202

title: Model Validation and Verification Guide
text: Several logical rules must hold true cost-effectiveness model. testhta facilitates writing assertions : Discounting: Total QALYs costs standard discounting (e.g., 3.5%) must strictly lower undiscounted QALYs costs. Extreme Discounting: discount rate extremely high (e.g. ∞\infty), future costs QALYs go zero. Utility Bounding: state utilities set 0, total QALYs must equal 0. state utilities set 1 discount rate 0, total QALYs must exactly equal life expectancy. Life Expectancy Consistency: Life expectancy affected changes cost utility values, change discount rate. Absorbing States: transition probabilities death state 1, life expectancy must exactly 1 cycle. transition probabilities death 0, life expectancy must equal time horizon (n_cycles).

title: Model Validation and Verification Guide
text: make writing unit tests readable succinct possible, testhta exports several test assertion helpers:

title: Model Validation and Verification Guide
text: functions check model outcomes single expected value: check_model_qalys(expected_qalys, data, ...) check_model_costs(expected_costs, data, ...) check_model_le(expected_le, data, ...) example, test QALYs exactly zero discount rate set 1 (100%): life expectancy exactly 1 cycle death probability 1:
code: data(test_data)  check_model_qalys(   expected_qalys = 0,   data = test_data,   discount_rate = 1,   label = "QALYs are 0 under 100% discount rate" ) check_model_le(   expected_le = 1,   data = test_data,   p_healthy_death = 1,   p_sick_death = 1,   label = "LE is 1 when death probability is 1" )

title: Model Validation and Verification Guide
text: Use compare_model_runs() test changing parameter shifts model outcomes expected direction: extractor_fn: Function get outcome (e.g., get_qalys, get_costs, get_le). comparison_fn: Comparison expectation (e.g., expect_lt, expect_gt, expect_equal). params_1: List parameter overrides run 1. params_2: List parameter overrides run 2. data: Base dataset. verify discounting reduces total costs:
code: compare_model_runs(   extractor_fn = get_costs,   comparison_fn = expect_lt,   params_1 = list(discount_rate = 0.035), # Run 1: discounted   params_2 = list(discount_rate = 0),     # Run 2: undiscounted   data = test_data,   label = "Discounted costs are less than undiscounted costs" )

what: 3. Integrating Validation into a Model Development Workflow
title: Model Validation and Verification Guide
text: developing modifying health economic model, place test files tests/testthat/ directory R package. example, create tests/testthat/test-model_validation.R: can execute tests point development running: automates verification process acts safety net accidental bugs updating transitions, costs, utility functions.
code: library(testthat) library(testhta)  data(test_data)  test_that("economic logic holds for cohort markov model", {   # Discounting logic   compare_model_runs(get_qalys, expect_lt, list(discount_rate = 0.035), list(discount_rate = 0), test_data)      # Life Expectancy logic   check_model_le(expected_le = 50, data = test_data, p_healthy_death = 0, p_sick_death = 0, n_cycles = 50)      # Zero costs logic   check_model_costs(expected_costs = 0, data = test_data, c_healthy = 0, c_sick = 0, c_intervention = 0, c_death = 0) }) devtools::test()

code: @Manual{,   title = {testhta: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models},   author = {Nathan Green},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://n8thangreen.github.io/testhta/}, }

title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: Unit Testing Verification Framework Health Technology Assessment (HTA) Models testhta provides robust, software-engineering-inspired framework applying unit testing behavioral verification health economic health technology assessment (HTA) models. leveraging testthat testing framework, testhta enables modelers write automated checks verify mathematical, logical, structural correctness boundary conditions. package includes built-cohort Markov model demonstration environment, showing define, group, execute checks practice. Ensuring HTA model quality require manual spreadsheet audits. testhta makes verification automated, reproducible, transparent—enhancing decision-maker confidence cost-effectiveness results.

title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: 🧪 Verification Helpers: ---box functions test model behavior expected bounds (check_model_qalys(), check_model_costs(), check_model_le()). 🔄 Relational Comparators: Compare model behaviors across parameter spaces using compare_model_runs() (e.g., verifying discounting reduces future values). 🔗 Tidy Test API: Modify parameters cleanly using setter functions (set_discount_rate(), set_time_horizon()) integrated base R pipe (|>) isolate test runs. ⚙️ Markov Engine Example Case Study: example discrete-time cohort Markov simulation environment built time-dependent transitions, discounting, state/transition-specific costs utilities demonstrate test framework.

title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: can install development version testhta GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("n8thangreen/testhta")

text: Easily perform scenario analyses sensitivity sweeps piping parameter modifications:
code: # Evaluate a 10-year time horizon with no discounting scenario_results <- test_data |>   set_time_horizon(10) |>   set_discount_rate(0) |>   run_model()  get_icer(scenario_results) #> [1] 12903.45

previous_headings: Quick Start: Build, Run, and Verify
title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: testhta provides assertion functions integrate seamlessly standard R package tests (using testthat).
code: library(testthat)  # Verify that QALYs with standard discounting (3.5%) are strictly less  # than QALYs without discounting compare_model_runs(   extractor_fn = get_qalys,   comparison_fn = expect_lt,   params_1 = list(discount_rate = 0.035),   params_2 = list(discount_rate = 0),   data = test_data,   label = "Discounted QALYs are lower than undiscounted QALYs" )

title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: validation checks testhta based formal software verification methodologies described Tappenden et al. (2014) Elbasha & Dasbach (2017). framework checks “black-box” model behavior extreme inputs identify structural bugs:

title: Validate Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-Analytic Models
text: Tappenden, P., & Chilcott, J. B. (2014). Avoiding Identifying Errors Threats Credibility Health Economic Models. PharmacoEconomics, 32, 967–979. https://doi.org/10.1007/s40273-014-0186-2 Elbasha, E. H., & Dasbach, E. J. (2017). Verification Decision-Analytic Models Health Economic Evaluations: Overview. PharmacoEconomics, 35, 673–683. https://doi.org/10.1007/s40273-017-0508-2 Alarid-Escudero, F., et al. (2019). Need Change! Coding Framework Improving Transparency Decision Modeling. PharmacoEconomics, 37(11), 1329–1339. https://doi.org/10.1007/s40273-019-00837-x

title: testhta 0.1.0
text: First release testhta validation framework cost-effectiveness models R.

title: testhta 0.1.0
text: get_costs(): Retrieve total discounted costs. get_icer(): Calculate Incremental Cost-Effectiveness Ratio. get_le(): Retrieve total Life Expectancy. get_qalys(): Retrieve total discounted Quality-Adjusted Life Years. set_costs(): Modify state-occupancy costs. set_discount_rate(): Modify model discount rates. set_p_matrix(): Modify transition probability arrays. set_time_horizon(): Modify simulation time horizon. set_utilities(): Modify state-specific utilities.

title: testhta 0.1.0
text: check_model_costs(): Assert expected costs bounding conditions. check_model_le(): Assert expected life expectancy bounding conditions. check_model_qalys(): Assert expected QALYs bounding conditions. compare_model_runs(): Assert directional logical relationships two model parameter configurations. run_model(): Core parameter-override wrapper execution underlying Markov engine.

title: testhta 0.1.0
text: Created professional pkgdown documentation website using Bootstrap 5 flatly Bootswatch theme. Added comprehensive documentation website search index. Added stylized hexagon logo (man/figures/logo.png). introduction: Setup, execute, inspect Markov cost-effectiveness models. validation-guide: design run unit tests verify model logic boundaries. Overhauled package metadata (DESCRIPTION, URLs, BugReports, License).
